description_long: This hospital building features a modern, geometric design with a series of interconnected rectangular and polygonal volumes. The exterior is clad in a light, grid-patterned material, likely concrete or stone panels, giving it a clean and institutional appearance. Several flat roofs are visible, some with open courtyards or cutouts, and there are multiple vertical elements that may serve as ventilation shafts or architectural features. Large window openings are distributed along the facades, providing natural light to the interior spaces. The structure includes a prominent overhang and a recessed entry area, suggesting a main entrance. The overall layout is sprawling and low-rise, typical of contemporary healthcare facilities designed for accessibility and efficient circulation.
description: A sprawling, modern hospital with geometric forms, light grid-patterned facades, flat roofs, and multiple courtyards and vertical features.
description_view_0: This view highlights the main entrance area with a prominent overhang and several cutouts in the roof, as well as the angular arrangement of the building's wings.
description_view_1: From this side, the elongated facade with large window openings and the layered, stepped rooflines are most visible.
description_view_2: This angle shows the rear or side of the hospital, emphasizing the flat roofs, vertical shafts, and a circular window feature.
description_view_3: This perspective reveals the complex, multi-volume layout and the integration of open courtyards and overhangs.
